title: Preprints Explained
script_summary: Explores what preprints are and their implications in science.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you that not all research is peer-reviewed?
Preprints are research papers shared before peer review. They spark debate and discovery.
But why are they controversial? Some argue they spread misinformation.
Yet, they accelerate scientific progress, allowing immediate feedback and collaboration.
So, preprints are a double-edged sword—informative but needing caution.
Follow for more insights on science and stay informed!
